canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, ADE20K is consistently framed as a benchmark or dataset for evaluation purposes. Its most prevalent use is to measure model performance on `Semantic segmentation`, a task for which multiple papers report using it. One paper specifically characterizes it as a "large-scale scene parsing benchmark" for assessing "open-vocabulary semantic segmentation" and notes its diverse object and stuff classes. The A-150 version of the benchmark is also explicitly mentioned. In addition to semantic segmentation, it is also used to measure `Image segmentation`. A less common framing describes it as a potential alternative dataset for a "patch-level probing benchmark," as noted in one paper which states, "we can also use other datasets like Ade20k".
papers: Decoupling Angles and Strength in Low-rank Adaptation
Maintaining Structural Integrity in Parameter Spaces for Parameter Efficient Fine-tuning
Revisit the Open Nature of Open Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation
Text4Seg: Reimagining Image Segmentation as Text Generation
Transformer Meets Twicing: Harnessing Unattended Residual Information

definitions.text: A large-scale scene parsing benchmark (A-150) used for evaluating open-vocabulary semantic segmentation with a diverse set of objects and stuff classes.
definitions.paper: Text4Seg: Reimagining Image Segmentation as Text Generation

definitions.text: A semantic segmentation dataset mentioned as an alternative dataset for the patch-level probing benchmark.

relationships.paper: DreamClear: High-Capacity Real-World Image Restoration with Privacy-Safe Dataset Curation
relationships.evidence: We evaluate the model’s performance on ADE20K (A-150) (Zhou et al., 2019), PASCAL Context 59 (PC-59) (Mottaghi et al., 2014), and PASCAL VOC 20 (PAS-20) (Everingham, 2009) datasets, using mIoU as the evaluation metric. (Section 4.5)

MambaTree: Tree Topology is All You Need in State Space Model
4M-21: An Any-to-Any Vision Model for Tens of Tasks and Modalities
VisionLLM v2: An End-to-End Generalist Multimodal Large Language Model for Hundreds of Vision-Language Tasks
iclr25: Text4Seg: Reimagining Image Segmentation as Text Generation
